lessons.title: Basics Review
lessons.context: Grammar: Greetings, Numbers 1-20, Colors. A welcome back party.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do we do at a welcome back party?
questions.hint: Think about when you see your friends after a long time!

questions.question_text: What can you see at a party?
questions.hint: Parties have colorful things to make them pretty!

questions.question_text: How do you feel at a welcome back party?
questions.hint: When you see your friends again, you feel good!

items.definition: A word to greet someone.
The number 12.
A color made from red and blue.
A table you use for work or study.
items.example: My books are on the desk.

items.definition: A greeting we say when we meet someone
The number that comes after eleven
A color made from red and blue mixed together
A table where we sit to write and study

steps.title: The Welcome Back Party
steps.text: Today is our welcome back party! I say hello to my friends. There are twelve balloons in our classroom. I sit at my purple desk. We are so happy to be back at school!
steps.scene_description: children at classroom party with colorful balloons and decorations

questions.question: How many balloons are in the classroom?
questions.locate_text: There are twelve balloons in our classroom.

questions.question: What color is the child's desk?
questions.locate_text: I sit at my purple desk.

questions.question: How do the children feel at the party?
questions.locate_text: We are so happy to be back at school!

steps.explanation: We use greetings to say hello and introduce ourselves to friends.
examples: Hi, I am seven years old.
Good morning, nice to meet you!

items.sentence: The book is at the desk.
items.explanation: Use 'on' for things on top of desks

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! Welcome back to school! Today we have a special party. Look at all the balloons! Can you count them? There are twelve balloons. Now, please sit down at your desks. Emma, I like your purple desk. It's so pretty!

questions.question: How many balloons are there?
What color is Emma's desk?
What kind of party is it?

steps.prompt: Let's practice talking about the welcome back party! Listen and repeat after me.

prompts.prompt: Say: Hello, my name is [your name].
Say: I have twelve balloons at the party.
prompts.expected_text: I have twelve balloons at the party.

prompts.prompt: Say: My desk is purple and I am happy.
prompts.expected_text: My desk is purple and I am happy.

questions.question_text: Hello, I ___ Emma.
What color is mentioned in our lesson?
How many balloons are at the welcome back party?
True or False: We say 'hello' when we meet our friends.
Where do students sit in the classroom?

lessons.title: Description Review
lessons.context: Grammar: Body, Clothes, Animals, Family. Describing a Mystery Person.

steps.instruction: Let's think about describing people! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: When we describe a person, what can we talk about?
questions.hint: Think about what you can SEE when you look at someone!

questions.question_text: If someone has long, yellow hair, what color is it?
questions.hint: Yellow hair has a special name. It starts with 'B'!

questions.question_text: What question do we ask to find out about someone's clothing?
questions.hint: We want to know about their shirt, pants, or dress!

items.definition: A part of your body with your hand.
A piece of clothing for the top of your body.
An animal with a long neck.
A girl child of a mother or father.

items.hint: This is part of your body. You use it to wave!
You wear this on top. It has sleeves!
This animal is very tall with a long neck!
A girl in a family. Mom and Dad's child!

items.sentence: I can wave my ___ to say hello!
Tom is wearing a blue ___ today.
Look at that tall ___ eating leaves from the tree!
This is my ___. Her name is Emma.

steps.title: Who Is She?
steps.text: My daughter Emma is playing in the park. She is wearing a yellow shirt. Look! She is waving her arm at a tall giraffe toy. Can you find her?
steps.scene_description: young girl in yellow shirt waving at toy giraffe in playground

questions.question: Who is playing in the park?
questions.locate_text: My daughter Emma is playing in the park. She is wearing a yellow shirt.

questions.question: What color is Emma's shirt?
questions.locate_text: She is wearing a yellow shirt.

questions.question: What is Emma waving?
questions.locate_text: She is waving her arm at a tall giraffe toy.

steps.explanation: We use 'has' to describe what a person looks like.
examples: She has long hair and blue eyes.
He has brown eyes and a red shirt.
My friend has short hair and a big smile.

items.correctSentence: He is wearing a blue shirt
The giraffe has a long neck

steps.audio_text: Hello, children! Today I want to tell you about my daughter, Lucy. She is seven years old. She loves animals, especially giraffes! Right now, she is wearing her favorite green shirt. Can you wave your arm like Lucy does when she sees a giraffe at the zoo?

questions.question: What is the teacher's daughter's name?
What color is Lucy's shirt?
What is Lucy's favorite animal?

steps.prompt: Let's practice describing people! Listen and repeat after me.

prompts.prompt: Say: He is wearing a blue shirt.
prompts.expected_text: He is wearing a blue shirt.

prompts.prompt: Now describe someone you know! Say: My [mom/dad/friend] has [color] [hair/eyes].

questions.question_text: Which body part do you wave?
She ___ a red shirt today.
Read: 'My daughter has blue eyes and long hair.' What color are her eyes?
True or False: A giraffe has a long neck.
Complete: He ___ brown hair.

lessons.title: Usage Review
lessons.context: Grammar: Present Continuous, Can/Can't, Likes/Dislikes. A weekend trip story.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do people go on a weekend trip?
questions.hint: Think about where families go to have fun together!

questions.question_text: What do you take with you on a trip?
questions.hint: You carry your clothes and toys in this!

questions.question_text: How do you feel when you go on a trip?
questions.hint: Trips are fun! How does something fun make you feel?

items.definition: Taking in food.
To be able to do something.
When you enjoy something.
The room where we make food.

steps.title: The Beach Trip
steps.text: It's Saturday! Emma and her family are going to the beach. They are eating sandwiches in the kitchen. Emma likes ice cream. She can swim in the sea. They are very happy!
steps.scene_description: happy family eating together in bright kitchen preparing for beach trip

questions.question: Where is Emma's family going?
questions.locate_text: Emma and her family are going to the beach. They are eating sandwiches in the kitchen.

questions.question: What can Emma do?
questions.locate_text: She can swim in the sea. They are very happy!

questions.question: Where are they eating sandwiches?
questions.locate_text: They are eating sandwiches in the kitchen.

steps.rule_pattern: I am ___ ing. / I can ___. / I like ___.
steps.explanation: We use 'am/is/are + ing' to say what is happening now, 'can' to say what we are able to do, and 'like' to say what we enjoy!
examples: I can swim in the pool.

items.sentence: I can swims in the pool.
We are play in the kitchen.
items.explanation: Use 'are playing' for actions happening now

steps.audio_text: Hello! Let me tell you about Tom's weekend trip. On Saturday, Tom and his family went to the beach. In the morning, they were eating pancakes in the kitchen. Tom likes swimming, and he can swim very well! He swam in the sea all day. It was a great trip!

questions.question: Where did Tom go on the weekend?
What were they eating in the kitchen?
What can Tom do very well?

steps.prompt: Let's talk about a weekend trip! Say these sentences about what you can do and like.

prompts.prompt: Say: I like ___. (Choose something you like!)
Say: I can ___. (Tell me something you can do!)
Say: I am eating ___. (What are you eating now or what do you eat?)

questions.question_text: What do we do with food?
I ___ swim in the pool.
She ___ ice cream and cake.
Where do we cook food at home?
True or False: 'They is eating' is correct.

lessons.title: Starters Mock Exam
lessons.topic: The Island Adventure - Full Mastery Check
lessons.context: The Island Adventure where students use all skills to find a treasure and earn their Stage 2 Certificate.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do you think an island adventure happens?
questions.correct_answer: On an island in the sea
options: On an island in the sea
questions.hint: Think about where you find sand, water, and palm trees!

questions.question_text: What fun things can you do on an island?
questions.correct_answer: Swim and play on the beach
options: Swim and play on the beach
questions.hint: Islands have water all around them. What do you do in water?

questions.question_text: Who might you meet on an island adventure?
questions.hint: Adventures are exciting! You can meet people and see sea creatures!

items.definition: What you say when you meet someone
I ___ jump! (am able to)
Having food in your mouth
Clothes you wear on top
A tall animal with a long neck
The room where you cook food
The number after eleven
A table where you write and study
A friendly greeting word
This word means you are able to do something
When you put food in your mouth and chew
You wear this on your body with buttons
This animal has spots and eats leaves from trees
This room has a stove and refrigerator
This number comes after eleven and before thirteen
You sit at this to do your homework

items.question: What do you say when you meet a friend?
I ___ swim in the pool. Which word fits?
What am I doing when I have lunch?
What do you wear on your body with sleeves?
Which animal has a very long neck?
Where do you cook food at home?
Where do you sit to write and study?

steps.title: The Island Treasure
steps.text: Sam and Lily go to a small island. They can see a map under a big tree. The map shows where the treasure is. They walk to the beach and find a box. Inside the box, there are gold stars and a certificate. Sam and Lily are very happy!
steps.scene_description: Two young children discovering a treasure chest on a sunny beach with palm trees

questions.question: Where do Sam and Lily go?
questions.locate_text: Sam and Lily go to a small island. They can see a map under a big tree.

questions.question: What do they find under the big tree?
questions.locate_text: They can see a map under a big tree.

questions.question: Are Sam and Lily happy at the end?
questions.locate_text: Sam and Lily are very happy!

steps.explanation: We use 'can' to talk about things we are able to do.
examples: I can swim in the sea.

items.sentence: There ___ twelve pencils on the desk.

items.explanation: After 'can' use the base form: swim

items.sentence: The shirt are on the desk.

items.explanation: After 'can' use the base form: find

items.question: She ___ eating in the kitchen.
items.explanation: Use 'is' with she for present continuous

items.question: There ___ twelve pencils on the desk.

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! Today we are going on an island adventure. We can find a treasure on the beach. There are twelve gold stars in the treasure box. Let's all say hello to the island and start our adventure!

questions.question: Where can we find the treasure?
How many gold stars are in the treasure box?
What do we say to the island?

steps.prompt: Let's talk about our island adventure! Listen and repeat, then say your own sentences.

prompts.prompt: Say: I can find the treasure.
Say: We are going to the island.
prompts.expected_text: We are going to the island.

prompts.prompt: Now tell me: What can you do on the island?
prompts.expected_text: I can swim. / I can play. / I can find treasure.

questions.question_text: I ___ swim in the sea.
What number comes after eleven?
She ___ eating lunch in the kitchen.
True or False: A giraffe has a long neck.
Where do you find treasure on the island?
